rationale: The citing paper does more than list the reference: it explicitly uses Cornil and Chandon (2016) to support a core theoretical claim that reducing portion size of indulgent food need not reduce, and can even increase, anticipated taste enjoyment. That evidence is used in hypothesis development for the perceived trade-off between lower caloric density and smaller portions, and the paper later positions itself as bridging this portion-size stream. Although it does not reuse the multisensory imagery intervention, the cited paper materially informs the conceptual framing.
relation_description: The citing study draws on Cornil and Chandon (2016) as prior empirical evidence about the size-pleasure relationship in portion choices, using it to motivate predictions that smaller portions can preserve taste relative to lower-caloric-density alternatives. In this way, the cited paper functions as part of the conceptual foundation for the citing paper's framework.
